title: Sustaining & Growing Army Talent
description: Leveraging the varied skills and perspectives of a multifaceted workforce enhances organizational performance. The U.S. Army can drive innovation by fostering an inclusive environment.
text:
Electronic warfare and spectrum management considerations for small uncrewed aircraft systems in divisional brigades
The authors assessed challenges and opportunities related to integrating small uncrewed aircraft systems with electronic warfare capabilities in divisional brigades and identified ways to make XVIII Airborne Corps units more effective in meeting spectrum management challenges.
Options to improve acquisition and accountability for small uncrewed aircraft systems in divisional brigades
The authors assessed the implications of integrating additional uncrewed aircraft systems (UASs) for reconnaissance, fires, and other purposes below the division level. They derived implications for institutional and unit training and other institutional functions needed for success.
Small Uncrewed Aircraft Systems in Divisional Brigades
In this overview volume of a five-part series, the authors present their findings on how the U.S. Army can improve its training, acquisition, and use of small uncrewed aircraft systems to better prepare for combat operations.
Understanding the Link Between the Army Combat Fitness Test and Personnel Health and Injuries
One of the Army's stated goals for the Army Combat Fitness Test (ACFT) was to reduce preventable injuries. While there are limited data available, research suggests that the ACFT could lead to a long-term reduction in injury rates. Researchers recommend policy actions to reduce injuries and monitor soldiers at risk.
Reassessing the Basic Allowance for Housing for Army Personnel in a Rapidly Changing Housing Market
Rising housing prices and inflation since 2020 have brought attention to the adequacy of the Basic Allowance for Housing (BAH) for military personnel. RAND researchers assessed the adequacy of BAH and the BAH rate-setting methodology from the standpoint of Army personnel, in the context of recent changes in the housing market.
The Impact of Mindfulness Meditation Programs on Performance-Related Outcomes
This report presents results from a systematic review and meta-analyses of research examining how mindfulness meditation affects 13 performance-related outcomes of interest to the U.S. Army and broader military.
Independent Review of the Army Combat Fitness Test
The U.S. Army has a new fitness test for the first time in 40 years. A review finds that more evidence is needed to prove that all six test events adequately predict performance on combat tasks and that combat standards are a necessary metric for all Army jobs. Women, National Guard troops, and others are passing the test at lower rates.
Forecasting Religious Affiliation in the United States Army
Changes in the religious composition of the United States could affect the religious composition of recruits into the U.S. Army. This, in turn, could significantly alter the religious needs of the Army population. How has the religious composition of enlisted soldiers and of officers changed over time?
Soldier Preferences and Retention Effects of Changes in Army Reserve Training Requirements
Soldiers in the U.S. Army Reserve have traditionally been required to attend 39 days of training per year. However, across the readiness cycle, units may have their requirements changed with minimal notice. How do changes in training requirements affect a soldier's interest in staying in the Reserves?
